message:
feat(core): resolve table_or_subquery_result_column_names through attached schemas

Extend the TableStar resolution path inside
table_or_subquery_result_column_names to route schema-qualified
Table references (e.g. `aux.t`) through the attached connection
instead of always consulting the main schema.  Before this
commit, `SELECT * FROM aux.t` would emit the main-schema
column list for a table named `t` when it existed (or fall back
to the raw name when it didn't), not the aux-schema column
list.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- table_or_subquery_result_column_names() Table branch
  previously did:
    TableOrSubquery::Table { name, .. } => {
        self.named_relation_result_column_names(
            &name.name, visible_ctes, active_ctes
        )
    }
  which only ever looked at the bare name and never consulted
  name.schema.

- New match on name.schema.as_deref():
    None → bare-schema lookup as before.
    Some(schema_name) if is_builtin_schema(schema_name) → same
        bare-schema lookup (main, temp, and the few reserved
        schema names all resolve through the main connection).
    Some(schema_name) → route through
        self.with_attached_connection(schema_name, |attached| {
            Ok(attached.named_relation_result_column_names(
                &name.name, visible_ctes, active_ctes
            ))
        })
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![name.name.clone()])
    The fallback to [name.name] matches the existing behavior
    for unresolved tables and lets the caller error out later
    with a precise "column not found" message instead of
    returning an empty column list.

- The inline doc comment on the match explains the reason for
  the explicit arm structure vs. the tempting
    name.schema.as_deref().map_or_else(..., ...)
  form: map_or_else would require two closures both wanting
  `&mut *active_ctes`, and Rust can only have one live mutable
  borrow at a time.  The explicit match keeps the borrow
  scoped to a single arm.

- Two new end-to-end regression tests:

    test_prepare_select_star_from_attached_schema_uses_attached_column_names_when_main_matches
        - Open in-memory connection.
        - Create main.t(main_only INTEGER).
        - ATTACH ':memory:' AS aux.
        - Create aux.t(aux_id INTEGER, aux_value TEXT).
        - Prepare `SELECT * FROM aux.t;` and assert:
            column_count == 2
            column_names == ["aux_id", "aux_value"]
          (before this commit the prepare would report
          ["main_only"] from the main-schema table, since
          the resolver always keyed on the bare name.)

    test_create_table_as_select_star_from_attached_schema_uses_attached_column_names_when_main_matches
        - Same setup, then execute
          `CREATE TABLE copy AS SELECT * FROM aux.t WHERE 0;`
          and inspect `PRAGMA table_info(copy);` to assert the
          derived table's columns are aux_id + aux_value.  Pins
          the full CTAS path against the same bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
